post_state_files.test_stringkit.py:
import pytest
from stringkit import reverse, count_vowels, title_case, starts_with_vowel

# ==============================================================================
# Test reverse(s: str)
# ==============================================================================

@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_str, expected_output", [
    ("hello", "olleh"),
    ("python", "nohtyp"),
    ("racecar", "racecar"),  # Palindrome
    ("a", "a"),               # Single character
    ("", ""),                 # Empty string
    ("12345", "54321"),       # Numbers
    ("MixedCase", "esaCekciM"), # Mixed case
])
def test_reverse_normal_cases(input_str, expected_output):
    """Tests the reverse function with various inputs."""
    assert reverse(input_str) == expected_output

# ==============================================================================
# Test count_vowels(text: str)
# ==============================================================================

@pytest.mark.parametrize("text, expected_count", [
    ("hello world", 3),  # e, o, o
    ("aeiouAEIOU", 10),   # All vowels, mixed case
    ("rhythm", 0),        # No vowels
    ("Apple Pie", 4),     # A, e, i, e
    ("Programming", 3),  # o, a, i
    ("", 0),              # Empty string
    ("Vowels are fun", 5), # o, e, a, e, u
])
def test_count_vowels_various_inputs(text, expected_count):
    """Tests the count_vowels function with different inputs."""
    assert count_vowels(text) == expected_count

# ==============================================================================
# Test title_case(text: str)
# ==============================================================================

@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_text, expected_output", [
    ("hello world test", "Hello World Test"), # Normal case
    ("a single word", "A Single Word"),       # Single word
    ("already Title Case", "Already Title Case"), # Already correct
    ("  leading and trailing spaces  ", "Leading And Trailing Spaces"), # Spacing handling
    ("multiple   spaces here", "Multiple Spaces Here"), # Multiple spaces
    ("", ""),                                  # Empty string
    ("---", "---")                            # Non-alphabetic characters (treated as single word)
])
def test_title_case_various_inputs(input_text, expected_output):
    """Tests the title_case function."""
    assert title_case(input_text) == expected_output

# ==============================================================================
# Test starts_with_vowel(word: str)
# ==============================================================================

@pytest.mark.parametrize("word, expected_result", [
    ("apple", True),      # Starts with vowel (a)
    ("Orange", True),     # Mixed case vowel start
    ("banana", False),    # Starts with consonant (b)
    ("rhythm", False),    # Starts with consonant (r)
    ("A", True),          # Single character vowel
    ("B", False),         # Single character consonant
    ("", False),          # Empty string (Edge case)
    ("123", False),       # Starts with non-letter (Edge case)
])
def test_starts_with_vowel_various_inputs(word, expected_result):
    """Tests the starts_with_vowel function."""
    assert starts_with_vowel(word) == expected_result
